skill.name: ad-creative
skill.description: Generate and iterate ad copy, creative angles, and visual direction for paid advertising platforms. Supports Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, Twitter/X, and performance-data iteration.
skill.summary: Generate platform-ready ad creative and iterate from performance data.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from marketing terms, platform acronyms, Markdown links, and documentation examples. The remaining concern is publishable medium risk: the skill includes command examples, API calls, local CLI workflows, and API key placeholders that should only run with explicit user approval.

medium_findings.title: Credentialed API and CLI Examples Require User Approval
medium_findings.description: The reference documentation includes curl, git, npx, ffmpeg, and platform CLI examples. These are legitimate ad-production examples, but they can make network requests, use API keys, install or run tools, and incur service costs if executed automatically. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for operational risk, with no evidence of malicious intent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible Markdown examples and workflow guidance, not hidden code. Risk is still meaningful because following them may send requests with credentials or run local tools.

medium_findings.title: API Key Placeholders in Request Headers
medium_findings.description: The examples place GEMINI_API_KEY and ELEVENLABS_API_KEY into HTTP headers for documented provider APIs. This is normal API usage, but agents should avoid echoing secrets into logs or sending them to untrusted endpoints. Verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW because the examples are legitimate but credential-bearing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly shows API key environment variables used in outbound requests. The endpoints appear to be normal provider APIs, so this is a controlled-use concern rather than confirmed exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography and Reconnaissance Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography and reconnaissance terms in eval prompts, ad-platform references, and acronyms such as RSA for Responsive Search Ads. No cryptographic implementation, system enumeration logic, or malicious reconnaissance instruction was found in the reviewed context. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is marketing and advertising terminology, not code. Reviewed lines describe ad character limits and expected outputs, so the scanner pattern does not represent a security issue.

low_findings.title: Narrow Local File Reference Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to read product marketing context files if present and links to relative tool documentation. This is limited workspace context access, not path traversal or arbitrary filesystem access. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for traversal, with a minor privacy note for local context files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are fixed relative paths and documented context files. I found no instruction to enumerate directories, bypass boundaries, or access arbitrary user files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Blocks in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown code blocks include shell commands for API calls, local builds, video processing, and rendering. These examples should be treated as user-approved actions, not automatic execution steps.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly present and could run external programs or network requests. They are examples rather than concealed execution logic, which limits the severity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Network Endpoints in Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The references contain public documentation and API URLs for ad creative tools. These URLs are expected for a tool guide, but outbound calls should be limited to user-selected providers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network destinations are visible and related to documented providers. I found no evidence that the skill sends data to unrelated or suspicious endpoints.

content.value_statement: Ad teams need fresh copy and visuals that fit each platform. This skill creates structured ad variations, validates limits, and supports iteration from performance data.
actual_capabilities: Generates headlines, descriptions, primary text, and full ad variations by platform.
Checks copy against character limits for Google, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, and Twitter/X.
Organizes creative by angle, audience, offer, and upload-ready format.
Analyzes provided performance data to identify winning and weak creative patterns.
Suggests visual, video, and voiceover directions using documented generative tool options.
Supports batch generation workflows for multi-platform campaigns.
limitations: It does not replace platform policy review or regulated-industry legal review.
It needs accurate product, audience, offer, and performance context for strong outputs.
It does not directly launch campaigns unless separate approved tools are available.
Generated claims and statistics must be verified before use in live ads.

use_cases.title: Launch a New Paid Campaign
use_cases.description: Create first-round creative angles, copy variations, and visual directions for a new product or offer.

use_cases.title: Refresh Underperforming Ads
use_cases.description: Use CTR, conversion rate, CPC, or ROAS data to create new variations from winning patterns.

use_cases.title: Scale Multi-Platform Creative
use_cases.description: Adapt one message into platform-specific copy for Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, and Twitter/X.

prompt_templates.title: Create Starter Ads
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new campaign with basic product context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate ad creative for [platform]. Product: [product]. Audience: [audience]. Offer: [offer]. Create 3 angles with copy that fits platform limits.

prompt_templates.title: Build Google RSA Variations
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for search ads with keyword intent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Google responsive search ads for keyword [keyword]. Include 15 headlines, 4 descriptions, character counts, angle labels, and suggested pinning only when necessary.

prompt_templates.title: Iterate From Performance Data
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt using campaign performance metrics.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this ad performance data: [paste data]. Identify winning patterns, weak angles, and create new variations that extend the strongest themes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Scaled Creative Production
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for multi-platform copy and visual generation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-platform ad creative plan for [campaign]. Include copy, visual concepts, platform specs, batch production workflow, and test priorities.

output_examples.input: Meta campaign for an AI writing assistant targeting B2B SaaS content managers.
output: Angle: Speed. Primary text: Write campaign briefs and blog drafts faster without losing quality.
Headline: Draft Blogs 5x Faster. Description: Built for B2B teams.
Visual direction: Side-by-side before and after content calendar with clear time savings.

output_examples.input: Google Ads RSA for a small business CRM keyword.
output: Headlines include keyword, benefit, proof, and CTA variations with character counts.
Descriptions focus on pipeline visibility, simple setup, and trial action.
The output flags any over-limit line and provides a trimmed alternative.

output_examples.input: Existing ads with CTR, CPC, and conversion rate by angle.
output: The report identifies high-CTR pain-point ads and high-conversion proof ads.
It recommends pausing weak feature-led copy and testing combined proof plus pain variants.
New creative is grouped by hypothesis so results can be measured clearly.

best_practices: Provide product, audience, offer, platform, and compliance constraints before asking for variations.
Ask for character counts and trimmed alternatives before moving copy into ad platforms.
Use performance data to guide iteration instead of changing every creative element at once.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated claims, numbers, or testimonials without source verification.
Do not ask for one generic ad set across all platforms without adapting format and intent.
Do not let the agent run API or CLI examples without explicit approval and credential review.

faq.question: Which ad platforms does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, and Twitter/X, with platform-specific copy limits.

faq.question: Can it create visual concepts?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests image, video, voiceover, and production directions, but it does not create assets by itself.

faq.question: Can it use campaign performance data?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide metrics such as CTR, CPC, conversion rate, or ROAS to guide new creative iterations.

faq.question: Does it upload ads to platforms?
faq.answer: No. It can format creative for upload, but live campaign changes require separate approved tools.

faq.question: Is it safe for regulated industries?
faq.answer: Use it with review. Legal, medical, financial, and other regulated claims need human compliance approval.

faq.question: Does it work in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.
